data.description:
## Why
Two zero-to-low-code investments that protect the design and cut review cost at the source.

## What
- R-3: document the stage-by-stage asymmetric-error invariant in docs/plan-review-gate.md (finder errs-toward-surface; verifier errs-toward-author; DET floor fails-open; claim gate fails-closed; novelty/completion floors fail-toward-KEEP) so future tuning cannot accidentally align two skepticisms in one direction. Add a floor-tuning/criteria-authoring checklist note.
- R-5 (SEQUENCE AFTER the criteria work in WS1-8): a longer-form Markdown authoring guide DERIVED from the criteria registry (affirmative rule + good/bad example per criterion), section-keyed by criterion id, kept in sync by the existing prompt/routing drift-parity gate. Discoverability: full URL in review-plan --help epilog + existing docs; coach output cites an ANCHORED deep-link to the criterion section (ESLint/Ruff/rustc pattern); AND a rebar explain <criterion-id> command across CLI/MCP/library (rustc --explain / ruff rule pattern). Anchored-refusal idiom: coaching cites the criterion by name so rejected ACs trace to a rule.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Asymmetric-error invariant documented + authoring/tuning checklist note
- [ ] Registry-derived authoring guide (longer-form Markdown, section-keyed), CI-parity-gated
- [ ] Anchored deep-links in coach output; --help epilog URL
- [ ] rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library
- [ ] Sequenced after WS1-8 (do not anchor rubrics about to be rewritten)
data.title: WS10: Docs + authoring surface (R-3, R-5)
